description: HP 83620A Synthesized Sweeper (10 MHz to 20 GHz), part of the HP 8360 series. Programmable over HP-IB (GPIB/IEEE-488) using the SCPI language (the default; CIIL and 8340-Compatible languages are also selectable via SYSTem:LANGuage). Commands follow the SCPI tree shown in Table S-1 'HP 8360 SCPI COMMAND SUMMARY'. Most settings are accessed under the default SOURce subsystem, so the leading ':SOURce' is optional/implied (e.g. ':FREQuency:CW' == ':SOURce:FREQuency:CW'). Numeric values accept an optional units suffix (HZ/KHZ/MHZ/GHZ for frequency, S/MS/US for time, DB/DBM/PCT for level/depth) and most accept MAXimum, MINimum, and where noted UP/DOWN. Boolean parameters accept ON|OFF|1|0. Querying replaces the set value with '?'. After *RST the instrument returns to the predefined state listed under *RST in the command summary. *IDN? returns 'HEWLETT-PACKARD,model,serial number,DD MMM YY'. Command set is common to the 8360 family (83620A/B, 83640A/B, 83650A/B, etc.).

identity.description: Returns an identifying string in the format HEWLETT-PACKARD,model,serial number,DD MMM YY, where model, serial number, and firmware revision of the synthesizer queried are returned.

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Returns the instrument identification string: HEWLETT-PACKARD,model,serial number,DD MMM YY.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the synthesizer to its predefined state (see the *RST list in the command summary). Sets INITiate:CONTinuous OFF.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the Status Byte, the Data Questionable Event Register, the Standard Event Status Register, the Standard Operation Status Register, the error queue, the OPC pending flag, and any other registers summarized in the Status Byte.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Sets and queries the value of the Standard Event Status Enable Register.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Queries the value of the Standard Event Status Register. This is a destructive read.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Operation complete command. The synthesizer generates the OPC message in the Standard Event Status Register when all pending operations (such as sweep or selftest) have finished.

commands.name: operation_complete_query
commands.description: Operation complete query. The synthesizer returns an ASCII '1' when all pending operations have finished.

commands.name: options
commands.description: Returns a string identifying any device options installed.

commands.name: learn
commands.description: Returns a long string of device-specific characters that, when sent back to the synthesizer, restores that instrument state.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recalls the instrument state from the specified memory register.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Stores the present instrument state in the specified memory register. An execution error occurs if you try to save state 0.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets and queries the value of the Service Request Enable Register.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Queries the Status Byte. This is a non-destructive read.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Performs the same function as the Group Execute Trigger command defined by IEEE 488.1.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Performs a full selftest, without data logging or looping, and returns an error code: 0 passed, 1 failed, 2 not run yet, 3 aborted, 4 cannot execute the test, 5 cannot execute (test skipped), -1 unrecognized result.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Causes the synthesizer to wait for the pending commands to be completed before executing any other commands. For example, 'TSW;*WAI' starts a sweep and waits until it completes before executing the next command.

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Causes the sweep in progress to abort and reset. If INITiate:CONTinuous is ON it immediately restarts the sweep. The pending operation flag (driving *OPC, *WAI, *OPC?) undergoes a transition once the sweep is reset.

commands.name: trigger_sweep
commands.description: Trigger sweep. Equivalent to ':ABORt;INITiate[:IMMediate]' - aborts the current sweep and immediately initiates a new one.

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Sets and queries the percentage of AM when the SOURce is INTernal. If a value is given in dB it is converted to percent. After *RST the value is 50%.

commands.name: am_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency (in Hz) of the internal AM source. After *RST the value is 1 kHz.

commands.name: am_internal_function
commands.description: Sets and queries the waveform of the internal AM source. After *RST the value is SINusoid.

commands.name: am_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the source of the AM modulating signal. After *RST the value is EXTernal.

commands.name: am_mode
commands.description: Controls the AM depth limits of the synthesizer. NORMal is selected at *RST.

commands.name: am_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the status of the AM modulation. After *RST the setting is OFF.

commands.name: am_type
commands.description: Sets and queries the type of AM modulation. After *RST the setting is LINear.

commands.name: am_units
commands.description: Sets and queries the default AM depth units. After *RST the value is PCT.

commands.name: calibration_am_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries the automatic modulator calibration switch. If ON, each time a frequency or power is changed CALibration:AM[:EXECute] is attempted.

commands.name: calibration_am_execute
commands.description: When AM is on and the synthesizer is in CW or manual mode, performs a modulator calibration (provided power sweep is not active).

commands.name: calibration_peaking_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries the automatic RF peaking function. If ON, a peak is done at regular intervals automatically. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: calibration_peaking_execute
commands.description: Performs a single RF (YIG) peaking operation.

commands.name: calibration_span_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries the automatic span calibration state. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: calibration_span_execute
commands.description: Performs a span (frequency) calibration.

commands.name: correction_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the user flatness/array correction state. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: correction_flatness
commands.description: Sets and queries up to 801 frequency-correction pairs (frequency, correction in dB).

commands.name: correction_flatness_points
commands.description: Queries the number of frequency-correction pairs currently loaded.

commands.name: correction_array
commands.description: Sets and queries the correction array (up to 1601 points of correction in dB).

commands.name: correction_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the correction source (ARRay or FLATness).

commands.name: frequency_cw
commands.description: Sets and queries the CW (fixed) output frequency. After *RST the value is (MAX+MIN)/2.

commands.name: frequency_cw_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the CW frequency is coupled to (tracks) the center frequency. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: frequency_center
commands.description: Sets and queries the center frequency of the swept range. After *RST the value is (MAX+MIN)/2.

commands.name: frequency_span
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency span of the swept range.

commands.name: frequency_start
commands.description: Sets and queries the start frequency of the swept range. After *RST the value is MINimum.

commands.name: frequency_stop
commands.description: Sets and queries the stop frequency of the swept range. After *RST the value is MAXimum.

commands.name: frequency_mode
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency mode of the synthesizer. After *RST the value is CW.

commands.name: frequency_manual
commands.description: Sets and queries the manual frequency (the locked-on frequency in manual sweep mode), within the start/stop limits.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: between current start and stop limits

commands.name: frequency_multiplier
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency multiplier used to display/program frequency when an external multiplier is used. After *RST the value is 1.

commands.name: frequency_multiplier_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the frequency multiplier is applied. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Sets and queries a frequency offset added to the displayed/programmed frequency. After *RST the value is 0.

commands.name: frequency_offset_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the frequency offset is applied. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: frequency_step
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency step (increment) size used with UP/DOWN. After *RST the step is calculated from the span.

commands.name: frequency_step_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the frequency step size is automatically calculated from the span. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: power_level
commands.description: Sets and queries the RF output power level. After *RST the value is 0 dBm.

parameters.name: power
parameters.range: specified power range of the instrument

commands.name: power_state
commands.description: Turns the RF output on or off. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: power_mode
commands.description: Sets and queries the power mode (FIXed level or power SWEep). After *RST the value is FIXed.

commands.name: power_center
commands.description: Sets and queries the power-sweep center level. After *RST the value is 0 dBm.

commands.name: power_span
commands.description: Sets and queries the power-sweep span. After *RST the value is 0 dB.

commands.name: power_start
commands.description: Sets and queries the power-sweep start value. After *RST the value is 0 dBm.

commands.name: power_stop
commands.description: Sets and queries the power-sweep stop value. After *RST the value is 0 dBm.

commands.name: power_slope
commands.description: Sets and queries the power slope (vs frequency) compensation. After *RST the value is 0.

commands.name: power_slope_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the power slope compensation is applied. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: power_offset
commands.description: Sets and queries the power equation offset (added to the displayed/programmed power).

commands.name: power_offset_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the power offset is applied.

commands.name: power_attenuation
commands.description: Sets and queries the step attenuator setting (option-dependent).

commands.name: power_attenuation_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the attenuator is coupled (automatically set with output level). After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: power_range
commands.description: Sets and queries the power meter range used for power leveling/calibration.

commands.name: power_search
commands.description: Sets and queries the power search (external leveling source calibration) mode.

commands.name: power_step
commands.description: Sets and queries the power step (increment) size used with UP/DOWN. After *RST the value is 10 dB.

commands.name: power_step_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the power step size is automatically determined. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: power_alc_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the Automatic Level Control (ALC) state.

commands.name: power_alc_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the ALC leveling point/source. After *RST the value is INTernal.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth
commands.description: Sets and queries the ALC loop bandwidth.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic ALC bandwidth selection. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: power_alc_cfactor
commands.description: Sets and queries the coupling factor used with external detector leveling. After *RST the value is 16 dB.

commands.name: power_amplifier_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the state of the (optional) output power amplifier/booster.

commands.name: power_amplifier_state_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic control of the output amplifier. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: power_units
commands.description: Sets and queries the default power units. After *RST the value is DBM.

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Sets and queries the peak FM deviation. After *RST the value is 1 MHz.

commands.name: fm_coupling
commands.description: Sets and queries the FM input coupling. After *RST the value is AC.

commands.name: fm_filter_hpass
commands.description: Sets and queries the FM AC high-pass filter bandwidth. After *RST the value is MAXimum.

commands.name: fm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency of the internal FM source. After *RST the value is 1 MHz.

commands.name: fm_internal_function
commands.description: Sets and queries the waveform of the internal FM source.

commands.name: fm_sensitivity
commands.description: Sets and queries the FM input sensitivity. After *RST the value is MAXimum.

commands.name: fm_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the FM source. After *RST the value is EXTernal.

commands.name: fm_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the FM state. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: pulse_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse generator frequency. After *RST the value is 500 kHz.

commands.name: pulse_period
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse period. After *RST the value is 2 us.

commands.name: pulse_width
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse width. After *RST the value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulm_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse modulation state. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: pulm_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse modulation source. After *RST the value is INTernal.

commands.name: pulm_external_polarity
commands.description: Sets and queries the external pulse input polarity. After *RST the value is NORMal.

commands.name: pulm_external_delay
commands.description: Sets and queries the external pulse delay. After *RST the value is MINimum.

commands.name: pulm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the internal pulse modulation frequency. After *RST the value is 500 kHz.

commands.name: pulm_internal_period
commands.description: Sets and queries the internal pulse period. After *RST the value is 2 us.

commands.name: pulm_internal_width
commands.description: Sets and queries the internal pulse width. After *RST the value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulm_internal_gate
commands.description: Sets and queries the internal pulse gating state. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: pulm_internal_trigger_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the internal pulse trigger source. After *RST the value is INTernal.

commands.name: pulm_slew
commands.description: Sets and queries the pulse modulation rise time / slew. After *RST the value is MINimum.

commands.name: pulm_slew_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic pulse modulation rise-time selection. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: modulation_output_source
commands.description: Sets and queries which internal modulation source (AM or FM) is routed to the modulation output.

commands.name: modulation_output_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether the internal modulation source is routed to the modulation output connector.

commands.name: modulation_state
commands.description: Queries the overall modulation state (whether any modulation is active).

commands.name: marker_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the on/off state of marker n (n is 1 to 5, default 1). After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: marker_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the frequency of marker n. After *RST the value matches FREQ:CENTer.

commands.name: marker_mode
commands.description: Sets and queries the marker mode for marker n. After *RST the value is FREQuency.

commands.name: marker_reference
commands.description: Sets and queries the reference marker used by marker n in delta mode.

commands.name: marker_delta
commands.description: Queries the difference between two markers.

commands.name: marker_amplitude_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the amplitude-marker state for marker n. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: marker_amplitude_value
commands.description: Sets and queries the amplitude-marker depth for marker n. After *RST the value is 2 dBm.

commands.name: marker_all_off
commands.description: Turns all markers off.

commands.name: initiate_continuous
commands.description: Sets and queries continuous sweep mode. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: initiate_immediate
commands.description: Initiates (triggers) a sweep immediately.

commands.name: trigger_immediate
commands.description: Generates an immediate trigger event.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the trigger source.

commands.name: trigger_output_delay
commands.description: Sets and queries the trigger output delay.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Sets and queries the sweep time. After *RST the value is MINimum.

commands.name: sweep_time_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries the automatic sweep time switch. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: sweep_time_llimit
commands.description: Sets and queries the fastest (lower-limit) sweep time used by auto sweep time. After *RST the value is 10 ms.

commands.name: sweep_dwell
commands.description: Sets and queries the per-point settling plus dwell time (stepped sweep). After *RST the value is 100 us.

commands.name: sweep_dwell_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic dwell-time calculation. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: sweep_points
commands.description: Sets and queries the number of points in a stepped sweep. After *RST the value is 11.

parameters.name: points
parameters.range: number of step points (MIN to MAX)

commands.name: sweep_step
commands.description: Sets and queries the stepped-sweep step size (a function of the current span). After *RST the value is (StopMAX-StartMIN)/10.

commands.name: sweep_generation
commands.description: Sets and queries the sweep generation type. After *RST the value is ANALog.

commands.name: sweep_mode
commands.description: Sets and queries the manual sweep mode switch. After *RST the value is AUTO.

commands.name: sweep_manual_point
commands.description: Sets and queries the step point number to lock on in manual stepped sweep. After *RST the value is 1.

parameters.name: point
parameters.range: 1 to the number of step points

commands.name: sweep_manual_relative
commands.description: Sets and queries the manual sweep position as a fraction of the sweep. After *RST the value is 0.50.

commands.name: sweep_marker_state
commands.description: Sets and queries whether markers define the swept range. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: sweep_marker_xfer
commands.description: Transfers markers to the sweep range: marker 1 becomes start, marker 2 becomes stop.

commands.name: sweep_trigger_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the stepped-sweep trigger source.

commands.name: sweep_control_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the dual-source (master/slave) sweep control mode state.

commands.name: sweep_control_type
commands.description: Sets and queries the dual-source sweep control type (master or slave).

commands.name: list_frequency
commands.description: Sets and queries the list of frequencies (up to 801 points) used in LIST frequency mode.

parameters.name: frequencies
parameters.range: up to 801 points within specified freq range

commands.name: list_frequency_points
commands.description: Sets and queries the number of frequency points in the list.

commands.name: list_dwell
commands.description: Sets and queries the list dwell time(s) (up to 801 values). After *RST the value is 100 us (MINimum).

commands.name: list_dwell_points
commands.description: Queries the number of dwell-time points in the list.

commands.name: list_manual
commands.description: Sets and queries the list point number to lock on in manual list mode. After *RST the value is 1.

parameters.name: point
parameters.range: 1 to maximum number of defined points

commands.name: list_mode
commands.description: Sets and queries the list sweep mode. After *RST the value is AUTO.

commands.name: list_power_correction
commands.description: Sets and queries per-point power correction levels for the list (up to 801 values). After *RST the value is 0.

commands.name: list_power_correction_points
commands.description: Queries the number of power-correction levels in the list.

commands.name: list_trigger_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the list trigger source. After *RST the value is IMMediate.

commands.name: roscillator_source
commands.description: Sets and queries the reference oscillator source.

commands.name: roscillator_source_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic selection of the reference oscillator source. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Turns the front-panel display on or off. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: status_operation_event
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the Operation Status Event register.

commands.name: status_operation_condition
commands.description: Queries the Operation Status Condition register.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Sets and queries the Operation Status Enable register.

commands.name: status_operation_ntransition
commands.description: Sets and queries the Operation Status negative-transition filter.

commands.name: status_operation_ptransition
commands.description: Sets and queries the Operation Status positive-transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_event
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the Questionable Status Event register.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition
commands.description: Queries the Questionable Status Condition register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Sets and queries the Questionable Status (SRQ) Enable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_ntransition
commands.description: Sets and queries the Questionable Status negative-transition filter.

commands.name: status_questionable_ptransition
commands.description: Sets and queries the Questionable Status positive-transition filter.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Presets the status reporting (enable/transition) registers to their default values.

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Queries the SCPI version implemented by the instrument.

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Queries (dumps) the next entry from the instrument error queue.

commands.name: system_printer_dump
commands.description: Dumps the display/state to the configured printer.

commands.name: system_language
commands.description: Sets and queries the remote programming language.

commands.name: system_gpib_address
commands.description: Sets and queries the synthesizer HP-IB (GPIB) address. After *RST the value is 19.

commands.name: system_alternate
commands.description: Sets and queries the alternate (save/recall) register. After *RST the value is 1.

commands.name: system_alternate_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the alternate-register (alternate state) on/off. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Executes an instrument preset (factory or user, depending on SYSTem:PRESet:TYPE).

commands.name: system_preset_save
commands.description: Saves the current instrument state as the user preset state.

commands.name: system_preset_type
commands.description: Sets and queries the preset mode (factory or user).

commands.name: system_mmhead_select
commands.description: Sets and queries which millimeter-wave head connection is selected.

commands.name: system_mmhead_select_auto
commands.description: Sets and queries automatic millimeter-wave head selection. After *RST the value is ON.

commands.name: system_security_count
commands.description: Sets and queries the memory-clear (security) count. After *RST the value is 1.

commands.name: system_security_state
commands.description: Sets and queries the security (memory protection) state.

commands.name: system_key_assign
commands.description: Assigns a key code to a user menu key.

commands.name: system_key_clear
commands.description: Clears one or all user menu keys.

commands.name: system_key_disable
commands.description: Sets the save lock (disables the indicated front-panel function, e.g. SAVE).

commands.name: system_key_enable
commands.description: Clears the save lock (enables the indicated front-panel function, e.g. SAVE). After *RST the value is SAVE.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_execute
commands.description: Executes the specified selftest number.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_name
commands.description: Queries the name of the specified selftest number.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_points
commands.description: Queries the number of selftests available.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_result
commands.description: Queries the condition (result) of the selftests.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_enable
commands.description: Enables the listed selftests. After *RST all tests are enabled (ALL).

commands.name: diagnostics_test_disable
commands.description: Disables the listed selftests.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_loop
commands.description: Sets and queries selftest looping. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_log_state
commands.description: Sets and queries selftest result logging. After *RST the value is OFF.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_log_source
commands.description: Sets and queries when selftest results are logged. After *RST the value is FAIL.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_data_value
commands.description: Queries the measured value for a selftest data point.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_data_desc
commands.description: Queries the description for a selftest data point.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_data_maximum
commands.description: Queries the maximum limit for a selftest data point.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_data_minimum
commands.description: Queries the minimum limit for a selftest data point.

commands.name: diagnostics_test_continue
commands.description: Continues a paused selftest sequence.

commands.name: diagnostics_abus_average
commands.description: Sets and queries the number of ADC averages used by the analog bus measurements. After *RST the value is 1.

commands.name: diagnostics_abus_status
commands.description: Queries the analog-bus status.

commands.name: diagnostics_output_faults
commands.description: Queries the current instrument fault conditions.

commands.name: diagnostics_result
commands.description: Queries the diagnostic result.

commands.name: diagnostics_iorw
commands.description: Performs a diagnostic I/O read/write to the specified device number and value.

commands.name: diagnostics_instrument_pmeter_address
commands.description: Sets and queries the HP-IB address of the power meter used for calibration. After *RST/power-on the default is 13.

commands.name: diagnostics_instrument_printer_address
commands.description: Sets and queries the HP-IB address of the printer.
